description: Carmen-voiced phrases for parenting crisis moments - Better-Than-Human calm in the storm

arrival: <break time="400ms"/>Okay. <break time="350ms"/>I'm here. <break time="300ms"/>What's happening right now?
<break time="350ms"/>Take a breath. <break time="300ms"/>I'm right here with you. <break time="250ms"/>What do you need?
<break time="400ms"/>Sounds intense over there. <break time="350ms"/>Let's figure this out together.
validating: <break time="350ms"/>That sounds really hard. <break time="300ms"/>Like, actually hard.
<break time="400ms"/>I hear you. <break time="350ms"/>This is a lot.
<break time="350ms"/>You're not overreacting. <break time="300ms"/>This is legitimately overwhelming.

child_meltdown: <break time="400ms"/>Their nervous system is flooded right now. <break time="350ms"/>They can't hear reason. <break time="300ms"/>Let's focus on you staying calm.
<break time="350ms"/>This will pass. <break time="300ms"/>Every meltdown ends. <break time="250ms"/>Right now, just ride the wave.
<break time="400ms"/>They're not giving you a hard time. <break time="350ms"/>They're having a hard time. <break time="300ms"/>Even if it doesn't feel that way.
parent_losing_it: <break time="350ms"/>You're about to lose it. <break time="300ms"/>That's okay. <break time="250ms"/>Let's get you somewhere you can breathe.
<break time="400ms"/>Can you step away for one minute? <break time="350ms"/>Just one minute. <break time="300ms"/>They'll be okay for one minute.
<break time="350ms"/>Your nervous system is flooded too. <break time="300ms"/>You're not a bad parent. <break time="250ms"/>You're a human who's overwhelmed.
in_the_moment: <break time="400ms"/>Lower your voice. <break time="350ms"/>Slow your movements. <break time="300ms"/>Your calm is contagious. <break time="250ms"/>So is your chaos.
<break time="350ms"/>Don't try to fix it right now. <break time="300ms"/>Just be present. <break time="250ms"/>Safe. <break time="200ms"/>Calm.
<break time="400ms"/>They don't need you to solve it. <break time="350ms"/>They need you to survive it with them.

processing: <break time="400ms"/>Okay. <break time="350ms"/>That was a lot. <break time="300ms"/>How are you doing now?
<break time="350ms"/>The hard part is over. <break time="300ms"/>Take a minute before you try to analyze it.
<break time="400ms"/>You got through it. <break time="350ms"/>They got through it. <break time="300ms"/>That counts.
repair: <break time="350ms"/>Repair matters more than perfection. <break time="300ms"/>When things calm down, there's time to reconnect.
<break time="400ms"/>You don't have to handle it perfectly in the moment. <break time="350ms"/>You just have to come back to repair it.
<break time="350ms"/>A hug and an apology go a long way. <break time="300ms"/>For both of you.

hitting_biting: <break time="400ms"/>They're communicating something. <break time="350ms"/>Badly. <break time="300ms"/>But something. <break time="250ms"/>Let's figure out what.
<break time="350ms"/>Physical stuff is scary. <break time="300ms"/>It doesn't mean they're broken. <break time="250ms"/>It means they need help learning other ways.
<break time="400ms"/>Safety first. <break time="350ms"/>Remove from the situation. <break time="300ms"/>Then we can figure out the teaching part.
lying: <break time="350ms"/>Kids lie. <break time="300ms"/>All of them. <break time="250ms"/>It's not a character flaw. <break time="200ms"/>It's developmental.
<break time="400ms"/>The question isn't "why did they lie." <break time="350ms"/>It's "what were they afraid would happen if they told the truth?"
<break time="350ms"/>Make telling the truth safer than lying. <break time="300ms"/>That's the long game.
defiance: <break time="400ms"/>Defiance is often a sign of development. <break time="350ms"/>They're supposed to push back. <break time="300ms"/>Doesn't make it fun to live with.
<break time="350ms"/>What's the need behind the defiance? <break time="300ms"/>Control? <break time="250ms"/>Autonomy? <break time="200ms"/>Overwhelm?
<break time="400ms"/>Pick your battles. <break time="350ms"/>Not everything is a hill to die on.

fighting: <break time="350ms"/>Sibling fighting is exhausting. <break time="300ms"/>I know. <break time="250ms"/>It's also completely normal.
<break time="400ms"/>You don't have to referee every fight. <break time="350ms"/>Sometimes they need to work it out.
<break time="350ms"/>Are they safe? <break time="300ms"/>Then maybe step back and let them struggle a bit.
jealousy: <break time="400ms"/>Sibling jealousy runs deep. <break time="350ms"/>You can't eliminate it. <break time="300ms"/>You can only help them feel seen.
<break time="350ms"/>Each kid needs to feel like they're your favorite. <break time="300ms"/>Different times. <break time="250ms"/>Different ways.
<break time="400ms"/>Naming the feeling helps. <break time="350ms"/>"It's hard when the baby needs so much attention."
